nodes.name: initialize_glft_state
nodes.description: Initializes the state for the GLFT model coefficients.

outputs.name: initial_c1
outputs.constraints: Represents the initial value of the state variable 'c1'.

outputs.name: initial_c2
outputs.constraints: Represents the initial value of the state variable 'c2'.

nodes.conceptual_summary: initialize_2d_scalar_state. Generates a fixed, two-dimensional state vector represented by two independent scalar floating-point values. This function acts as a factory, providing a deterministic, hardcoded starting point for any system or model requiring a two-parameter initial condition.. Applications: Initializing the starting (x, y) coordinates for a particle in a 2D particle filter for robot localization., Setting the initial proportional and integral gain parameters for a PI controller in a feedback control system before an auto-tuning process begins., Defining the initial population sizes for two interacting species in a Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model., Establishing the starting weights for a two-neuron, single-layer perceptron in a simple neural network.

nodes.name: update_glft_coefficients
nodes.description: Updates the GLFT model coefficients based on market parameters and the previous state. This embodies the core state transition.

inputs.name: last_c1
inputs.constraints: The previous state of the 'c1' coefficient.

inputs.name: last_c2
inputs.constraints: The previous state of the 'c2' coefficient.

outputs.name: next_c1
outputs.constraints: The updated state of the 'c1' coefficient.

outputs.name: next_c2
outputs.constraints: The updated state of the 'c2' coefficient.

nodes.type_signature: (last_c1: float, last_c2: float, xi: float, gamma: float, delta: float, A: float, k: float) -> tuple[float, float]
nodes.conceptual_summary: iterative_coupled_scalar_update. Calculates the subsequent state of a 2-dimensional system from its preceding state. The transformation is a deterministic function of the two previous state components and a set of scalar control parameters, modeling a single step in a discrete-time evolution.. Applications: Simulating predator-prey population dynamics, where the two state variables represent the populations of two interacting species., Modeling the concentration of two chemical species in a reaction network over a discrete time step., Updating the position and velocity of a simulated physical object in a 2D physics engine, subject to forces defined by the control parameters., Evolving the activation level and fatigue state of a neuron in a computational neuroscience model.

nodes.name: evaluate_spread_conditions
nodes.description: Computes the half-spread from the current state and checks if it meets a validity condition against the c1 coefficient.
nodes.type_signature: (c1: float, c2: float, delta: float, volatility: float, adj1: float, threshold: float) -> tuple[float, bool]
nodes.conceptual_summary: gated_ratio_validity_check. Synthesizes a new scalar magnitude from a set of input parameters, including metrics for difference and variability. It then evaluates this derived magnitude by comparing its ratio with a primary input parameter against a predefined threshold, producing a boolean validity flag.. Applications: Control Systems: In a PID controller, evaluating if the derivative term (rate of change) is disproportionately large compared to the integral term (accumulated error) to detect potential instability., Signal Processing: Assessing the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) by comparing a derived noise metric (e.g., standard deviation) against the primary signal's amplitude to flag corrupted measurements., Epidemiology: Checking if the projected growth rate of new cases (derived from transmission and population metrics) exceeds a critical ratio relative to available healthcare capacity (e.g., hospital beds)., Materials Science: Predicting material fatigue by comparing a derived stress concentration value (calculated from load and geometry) to the material's intrinsic yield strength to determine if a failure threshold has been breached.

nodes.name: calculate_half_spread
nodes.description: Computes the half spread based on market coefficients, order size, and volatility.

inputs.name: adj1
inputs.constraints: Adjustment factor for the half spread

nodes.type_signature: (c1: float, c2: float, delta: float, volatility: float, adj1: float) -> float

nodes.name: validate_spread_to_c1_ratio
nodes.description: Checks if the ratio of the half-spread to the c1 coefficient is below a specified threshold, optimizing the balance between execution probability and adverse selection risk.

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.constraints: The ratio threshold for the heuristic check

outputs.name: is_valid_ratio
outputs.constraints: True if the ratio is within the acceptable threshold, False otherwise

nodes.type_signature: (half_spread: float, c1: float, threshold: float) -> bool
